title: HUD Moves to Limit Assistance Animals for Disabled Tenants
description: Housing officials were told to exclude emotional support animals and tighten the definition of a service animal when granting accommodations for disabled tenants.
author: Chris Cameron and Debra Kamin

title: Deadly Explosions Injure Dozens of Firefighters on Staten Island Barge
description: Two explosions rocked a barge on the North Shore of Staten Island, injuring 34 people. The resulting fire was under control, but was still burning.
author: Christopher Maag and Nicol León Arge

title: Green Card Seekers Must Leave U.S. to Apply, Trump Administration Says
description: The change is likely to affect hundreds of thousands of people. It could also lead to more family separations as spouses or relatives wait for application decisions, immigration lawyers said.
author: Madeleine Ngo and Albert Sun

title: Trump Just Took Us Somewhere the Country Had Never Been Before
description: There’s no telling where this leads.
author: Noah Shachtman

title: The Simple Answer to Taxing the Rich Is the Best Answer
description: Forget complex schemes. There is an easier way to get revenue from the wealthy.
author: Zachary Liscow

title: Stephen Colbert’s Last ‘Late Show’ Features Famous Friends and Paul McCartney
description: Colbert was joined by famous friends, fellow hosts and special guests like Paul McCartney for the finale of “The Late Show.”
author: Trish Bendix

title: Pope Leo Chooses Social Justice Over Pelvic Theology
description: The document will address A.I. and its impact on humanity. He’s also staying the course on Catholic social teaching.
author: David Gibson

title: San Diego Mosque Shooter So Alarmed Police, They Seized Father’s Guns
description: In 2025, Caleb Vazquez’s obsession with mass shooters and Nazism prompted reports to the authorities. The F.B.I. was also alerted to concerns about one of the teens.
author: Orlando Mayorquín, Tim Arango and Chelsia Rose Marcius

title: Lutnick Donated $5 Million to House Republicans Before Epstein Testimony
description: Howard Lutnick, the commerce secretary, made the donation after agreeing to an interview with lawmakers about his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.
author: Theodore Schleifer and Ana Swanson

title: Stephen Colbert’s Last ‘Late Show’: Laughing Well Is the Best Revenge
description: The “Late Show” cancellation was a disappointment. But a surreally lovely final episode turned it into a cancellebration.
author: James Poniewozik

title: The Civil Rights Era Is Collapsing Before Our Eyes
description: America is witnessing the greatest decimation of Black political power in over a century.
author: Nikole Hannah-Jones

title: Audit Immunity for Trump Family Puts I.R.S. in a Bind
description: Federal law prohibits the Internal Revenue Service from halting an audit at the direction of the president or his aides.
author: Andrew Duehren

title: GOP Pulls Measure on Trump’s War Powers in Iran, Lacking Votes to Defeat It
description: House Republican leaders abruptly scrapped a planned vote on a measure to direct President Trump to end the conflict or win authorization for it, amid party defections and absences.
author: Megan Mineiro, Robert Jimison and Michael Gold

title: Trump Says He Will ‘Try and Make’ Son Don Jr.’s Wedding, but Timing ‘Not Good’
description: President Trump said the war with Iran and “other things” would make it difficult for him to make the wedding this weekend.
author: Shawn McCreesh

title: Your Questions About Nicholas Kristof’s Column on Palestinians and Sexual Assault
description: The Times offers answers to some of the pressing questions raised.
author: New York Times Opinion

title: Ebola Is a Terrible Disease. I Saw It Firsthand.
description: The Trump administration cut the programs meant to coordinate an Ebola response.
author: Craig Spencer

title: Federal Panel Approves Trump’s Plans for a 250-Foot Arch in Washington
description: The panel, whose members were selected by President Trump, has an advisory role on the design of the project but no enforcement power.
author: Luke Broadwater

title: How the $1.8 Billion Trump Fund May Violate Past Practice and Policy
description: The fund that could offer payouts to Trump allies who accuse the government of mistreatment is not only highly unusual but also appears to violate the administration’s own policies.
author: Devlin Barrett

title: He Was Jailed Over a Charlie Kirk Post. The Sheriff Now Owes Him $835,000.
description: Larry Bushart sued a Tennessee sheriff who claimed he wanted to incite hysteria with a post after Mr. Kirk’s killing and jailed him for 37 days.
author: Rick Rojas

title: Trump’s Government Moves to Spare an Unhappy Taxpayer Named Trump
description: No president has ever used the federal government to advance his own personal interests and those of his family and allies as expansively and openly as Mr. Trump has.
author: Peter Baker
